title: Guard/quota hooks locate .switchboard/ by walking up from cwd, not relative to it
context: The verified Claude Code hook payload gives the hook the subagent's `cwd`. But a task subagent runs in its git WORKTREE (<repo>/.worktrees/<worker_id>), which has NO .switchboard/ — that dir is gitignored and exists only in the main working tree. The guard's per-agent state and the quota detector's quota.json must land in the MAIN repo's .switchboard/, not the worktree. The B spec assumed payload `cwd`/attribution but did not anticipate that cwd is the worktree. Getting this wrong silently writes state into the wrong (or a nonexistent) place.

options.name: walk-up-for-switchboard
options.rationale: find_root(cwd): walk parent directories from cwd until one contains a .switchboard/ dir; that is the engine root. Fail open (no-op) if none found.
options.tradeoff: One small upward-scan function; robust to worktree nesting depth and to being run from the main tree too.

options.name: git-common-dir
options.rationale: Run `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` to find the shared git dir (worktrees share it), derive the main worktree from there.
options.tradeoff: Authoritative for git worktrees, but shells out to git from inside a hook (latency on every tool call), and couples the hook to git — the engine is deliberately git-free.

options.name: assume-fixed-depth
options.rationale: Hardcode that cwd is exactly <repo>/.worktrees/<id> and go two levels up.
options.tradeoff: Simplest, but brittle: breaks the moment worktree layout/depth changes or the hook fires from the main tree (agent_id present but cwd == repo).

reasoning: An upward filesystem scan for the .switchboard/ marker is correct regardless of worktree depth or whether the call came from the main tree, needs no subprocess, and keeps the hook git-free (consistent with the engine's git-free principle). It is the cheapest robust option and fails open cleanly when no root is found. The same find_root is shared by both the guard and the quota hook to avoid drift.

steelman.strongest_case: It is the *authoritative* way to resolve a worktree to its main checkout — immune to a stray .switchboard/ dir somewhere above cwd, and it would also handle worktrees created outside the repo tree, which the upward scan would miss.
A's SKILL.md fixes the worktree at <repo>/.worktrees/<worker_id>, so depth is known today; the simplest code that works for the actual layout is arguably the leanest choice.

blast_radius: A single helper (find_root) in hooks/sb_quota.py, reused by hooks/sb_guard.py. Pure filesystem read. If a future layout breaks the upward scan (e.g. worktrees outside the repo tree), swap find_root for the git-common-dir approach — localized, one function. No engine or schema impact.

feedback.note: Approved the upward search, with a containment condition: it must have a hard limit on how far up it can climb, so a missing/misconfigured .switchboard cannot send it walking to the filesystem root or into another project. Incorporated as a max-parent-levels cap (default 16) in find_root, covered by tests/test_quota.py::test_find_root_walk_is_bounded.
